author: PHILdesign
notes: Complete assembled narrowbody airliner (wings, engines, tail, landing gear) at ideal marker poly count. Downloaded via Internet Archive's Thingiverse mirror (license metadata preserved there); binary STL, dims ~181x167x61 units.

author: RTicknor
notes: Single-mesh assembled widebody twin-jet, good generic silhouette. PROVENANCE CAVEAT: uploader states it is 'based off of' a SketchUp 3D Warehouse model, so the CC-BY-SA relicense may not be clean; prefer generic-a320.stl. Binary STL.

author: Jonah Ashton (Jonahash)
notes: Complete assembled 737-800 with winglets and engines, single mesh, ASCII STL (convert to binary to shrink). Same mesh is reposted as thing:5423098, also CC-BY.

author: Nathaniel Foster (Ndog262)
notes: Second 737-800 candidate; assembled single binary mesh, higher detail (engine fans, window insets). Heavier than the Jonahash mesh but still under 100k tris.

author: A. C. (Adcoff72)
notes: *** NC LICENSE - RISKY *** Only 757 obtainable: exhaustive search of Thingiverse/IA/GitHub/Wikimedia/NASA found NO freely-licensed 757 STL anywhere (other Thingiverse 757s are CC-BY-NC-ND; Printables/Sketchfab ones need auth). Excellent accurate assembled model with gear; ASCII STL, near the 100k-tri ceiling - decimate before use.

author: David Jimenez (AdmiralPumpkinPie24)
notes: *** NC LICENSE - RISKY *** Clean assembled 727 tri-jet with T-tail, ideal silhouette/poly count. GPL-2.0 alternative exists but only as OBJ, not STL: 'boeing 727' by Bogdan Deac (yuppy), thing:3452615, whole-plane boeing727.obj at https://archive.org/download/thingiverse-3452615/boeing_727_3452615.zip/files%2Fboeing727.obj - converting that OBJ to STL locally would give a redistribution-safe 727.
